title: Fagara cinerea
taxonomy: Fagara cinerea is classified as a species within the Rutaceae family, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This family is composed of flowering plants that are often aromatic and contain essential oils. Fagara cinerea is further classified as a genus within the Rutaceae family, and its specific classification is Fagara cinerea, with the family being Rutaceae and the order being Sapindales. The plant's classification places it within the broader group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Fagara cinerea's classification also reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Rutaceae family.
uses: Fagara cinerea, also known as the Chinese prickly ash, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The bark of the Fagara cinerea tree contains a compound called evodiamine, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Fagara cinerea is used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Additionally, the plant is also used to aid in weight loss and to improve blood circulation. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of Fagara cinerea.
benefits: Fagara cinerea, known for its medicinal properties, is believed to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. side_effects: Fagara cinerea contains coumarins, which may cause gastrointestinal upset, such as nausea and vomiting, when consumed in large quantities. It is also known to interact with blood thinners, potentially increasing the risk of bleeding. In traditional medicine, it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever and rheumatism. intro: Fagara cinerea, also known as Chinese prickly ash, is a plant species native to China, belonging to the Rutaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Fagara cinerea contains bioactive constituents like alkaloids, volatile oils, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
